name: Razorpay-Style Async Payment Dashboard
scenario: Build an async payment analytics dashboard that fetches transaction data from multiple regional payment APIs in parallel, processes the responses with Jackson, aggregates statistics, and persists results using NIO file I/O. This project synthesises the entire Module 11 arc: NIO files (T11.1), buffered I/O (T11.2), JSON processing (T11.3), HTTP client patterns (T11.4), and async parallel HTTP (T11.5).
description: A non-blocking payment aggregation system. Three regional APIs return JSON transaction arrays. sendAsync fires all three simultaneously. thenApply chains parse each response. allOf waits for all. Results are combined, validated, aggregated into per-status and per-region breakdowns, written to files using NIO, and displayed as a formatted PrintWriter report.

core.description: Static final HttpClient (sendAsync-ready) and ObjectMapper singletons. Define `Payment` record: id, region, status (SUCCESS/FAILED/PENDING), amount, currency, timestamp.

`fetchPaymentsAsync(String region)` → `CompletableFuture<List<Payment>>`: simulates sendAsync for that region, returns JSON array, parses with `TypeReference<List<Payment>>`. Each region has at least 4 payments and different delays (50-200ms).

Fan-out to 3 regions simultaneously. `allOf` waits for all. Inside `thenRun`: flatMap results into single list, compute: totalCount, successCount, failedCount, pendingCount, totalAmount (SUCCESS only), perRegionBreakdown.
Per-request error handling: each regional CF has `exceptionally(ex -> emptyList())` — one region failing doesn't break the others.

Write results using Module 11 I/O: (a) `Files.writeString` for JSON summary (Jackson ObjectNode), (b) `PrintWriter` wrapping `BufferedWriter` for formatted CSV of all payments, (c) NIO directory creation for output folder.
Print formatted console report using printf. Show timing: total async time vs estimated sequential time.
Total time should be approx max(region delays), not sum. Print the speedup ratio.

bonus.description: Add `thenCompose` chaining: after fetching all payments, fire a second async call to a 'reconciliation API' (simulated) that takes the failed payment IDs and returns a retry result. Chain: allOf → reconcile → finalReport.
Add retry with exponential backoff: if a regional API fails (throw exception in simulate), automatically retry up to 3 times with 100ms/200ms/400ms waits before falling back to empty list.
Read previous report from file (if exists) and compare: print delta in total payments, delta in success rate, and any new failed payment IDs since last run. Use Jackson to read old report.json.

data_to_use:
3 regions with payments:
North: PAY-001(SUCCESS, ₹1500), PAY-002(FAILED, ₹800), PAY-003(SUCCESS, ₹2200), PAY-004(PENDING, ₹650)
South: PAY-005(SUCCESS, ₹3500), PAY-006(FAILED, ₹450), PAY-007(SUCCESS, ₹999), PAY-008(SUCCESS, ₹1800)
West:  PAY-009(PENDING, ₹2200), PAY-010(SUCCESS, ₹750), PAY-011(FAILED, ₹1200), PAY-012(SUCCESS, ₹4999)

Delays: North=150ms, South=200ms, West=100ms
Expected total: 12 payments
Expected successful: 7 of 12
Expected total (SUCCESS only): 1500+2200+3500+999+1800+750+4999 = 15748
example_output:
=== Async Payment Dashboard ===
Querying 3 regional APIs in parallel...
[North API] → 4 payments in 151ms
[West API]  → 4 payments in 103ms
[South API] → 4 payments in 202ms

=== Aggregation Results ===
Total payments: 12
Successful:  7 (58.3%)
Failed:      3 (25.0%)
Pending:     2 (16.7%)
Success amount: ₹15,748

=== Regional Breakdown ===
North:  4 payments | 2 success | 1 failed | ₹3,700 success
South:  4 payments | 3 success | 1 failed | ₹6,299 success
West:   4 payments | 2 success | 1 failed | ₹5,749 success

Async time:       ~202ms
Estimated sequential: ~450ms
Speedup: 2.2x

Files written:
  output/summary.json
  output/payments.csv
ai_policy_explanation: Module 11 capstone — no AI assistance. All patterns covered in T11.1–T11.5. Key: fetchPaymentsAsync returns CF from supplyAsync simulating delay + JSON parse. exceptionally returns empty list. allOf.thenRun: flatMap three lists → aggregate. Files.writeString for JSON; PrintWriter for CSV.
submission_instructions: Save as PaymentDashboard.java. Push to GitHub. Commit message: 'Module 11 capstone: async payment dashboard (sendAsync + Jackson + NIO)'.
tips_for_success: fetchPaymentsAsync: `return CompletableFuture.supplyAsync(() -> { sleep(delay); return MAPPER.readValue(json, new TypeReference<List<Payment>>(){}); }).exceptionally(ex -> List.of())`
CSV output: PrintWriter prints one row per payment — wrap IOException as RuntimeException inside thenRun
Timing: record millis before firing futures, compute delta inside thenRun
concepts_reinforced: thenApply for JSON parsing in async chain
exceptionally per CF for graceful degradation
